introSection: Gebirgsbahn was a steel roller coaster located at Phantasialand in Brühl, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was named Bobbahn 1 when it originally opened and had no theming. The 1 in the name was followed with the opening of Bobbahn 2 opened three years later.

The ride received its rock theming and facade a year later, in 1976. This was done out of necessity; the coaster was too loud for neighbors of the area, and the rock facade and tunnels muted the sound so people living nearby wouldn't complain. The rides where eventually renamed to Gebirgsbahn and Grand Canyon Bahn in 1989.

On 1 May 2001, a short circuit in some wiring caused a fire that it was destroyed by. Grand Canyon Bahn and Tanakra, an animatronic show where also destroyed in this fire. The park would eventually replace the rides with River Quest and Feng Ju Palace respectively.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Grey Track with the supports hidden in the rock structure.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains were quite similar to those found on early Looping Star models, like Silver Bullet at Frontier City, and the original trains for Revolution at Six Flags Magic Mountain.

fullText:
Gebirgsbahn was a steel roller coaster located at Phantasialand in Brühl, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

The ride was named Bobbahn 1 when it originally opened and had no theming. The 1 in the name was followed with the opening of Bobbahn 2 opened three years later.

The ride received its rock theming and facade a year later, in 1976. This was done out of necessity; the coaster was too loud for neighbors of the area, and the rock facade and tunnels muted the sound so people living nearby wouldn't complain. The rides where eventually renamed to Gebirgsbahn and Grand Canyon Bahn in 1989.

On 1 May 2001, a short circuit in some wiring caused a fire that it was destroyed by. Grand Canyon Bahn and Tanakra, an animatronic show where also destroyed in this fire. The park would eventually replace the rides with River Quest and Feng Ju Palace respectively.



Grey Track with the supports hidden in the rock structure.

The trains were quite similar to those found on early Looping Star models, like Silver Bullet at Frontier City, and the original trains for Revolution at Six Flags Magic Mountain.
